leads.issues: Checking the raw HTML directly, the homepage's entire <head> section contains nothing but a <title> tag - no meta description, no Open Graph tags, no viewport meta tag, and no structured data of any kind. With no meta description written, whatever generates a search snippet falls back to scraping visible page text, and right now that produces: "The Toy Chest's Top 21 Newest Arrivals Shop by Brands The Toy Chest's Top 21 Recommended Treasures Lego: Icons- Ford Model T 11376 $129.99 Add to cart" - a jumble of section labels and one random product's price, not a description of the store, and it changes every time inventory rotates. Separately, the site has no About or Contact page anywhere in its navigation - just a flat product-category menu and a cart icon - so the shop's story, differentiators, and hours only exist as one paragraph in the header banner. In the "Shop by Brands" logo row, most brand logos (Duncan, Disney, and others) have empty alt attributes, and the DC Comics logo's alt text is literally the raw file name "DC_Comics_logo.svg" instead of a real description.
leads.opportunity: Writing a real meta description, adding Open Graph tags, and adding a mobile viewport meta tag are same-day fixes - literally a few lines in the theme header. Real alt text across the brand-logo row is a same-day fix too. Building out a proper About/Contact page with the shop's story, hours, and address is a same-week addition. Expected within 30 days: a stable, store-specific search snippet instead of one that changes with inventory and shows a random product's price, and better matching for "toy store Hope NJ" and "independent toy store Warren County" searches.
